titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 568

p.seg: David by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all throughout his kingdom settled in Scotia and Lothian, Gael and English, greeting.

Know me to have conceded, and given in alms, to God, Saint Cuthbert and his monks of Durham, these lands in Lothian, that is to say, Coldingham, Old Cambus, Lumsdaine, Reston, Renton, Swinewood, Prenderguest, Ayton and the other Ayton, and Cramsmoo, Lamberton and the other Lamberton, Paxton, Fishwick, and Swinton. All these lands written above I give and concede to God, and to the aforesaid saint and to his monks of Durham, with sake and soke, toll and team, and infangtheif, and with all lands, woods, waters, and shipwrecks, and with all customs, free and quit from every work and service, for the salvation of my own soul and of my son Henry, and for the souls of my father and mother, and my brothers and sisters. I also enjoin and forbid that anyone cause any injustice, trouble, or challenge in respect of this my donation to the monks of Saint Cuthbert of Durham henceforward, since I will that this my alms remain free and quit from every claim for ever.
This charter has been made firm in the year 1126 after the Lord's incarnation, in the third year of my reign, at Peebles,

by the witness and consent of Henry my son. And these others are witnesses of it: John the bishop, Robert de Brus, Herbert the chancellor, Ascelin the archdeacon, Payn de Briouze, Hugh Brett, Berengar Engaine, Cospatric the sheriff, Aimer.
